segments.text: Why hast thou served us thus, that thou calledst us not, when thou wentest to fight with the Midianites?
segments.speaker: Men of Ephraim

segments.text: And they did chide with him sharply.

footnotes: Why…: Heb. What thing is this thou hast done unto us

segments.text: What have I done now in comparison of you? Is not the gleaning of the grapes of Ephraim better than the vintage of Abi-ezer?
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: God hath delivered into your hands the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb: and what was I able to do in comparison of you?
Then their anger was abated toward him, when he had said that.
And Gideon came to Jordan, and passed over, he, and the three hundred men that were with him, faint, yet pursuing them.
Give, I pray you, loaves of bread unto the people that follow me; for they be faint, and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in thine hand, that we should give bread unto thine army?
segments.speaker: Princes of Succoth

segments.text: Therefore when the LORD hath delivered Zebah and Zalmunna into mine hand, then I will tear your flesh with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers.
segments.speaker: Gideon
